title: artemisia sacrorum subsp. iwayomogi
intro: Artemisia sacrorum subsp. iwayomogi is a plant species belonging to the genus Artemisia, commonly known as wormwood or mugwort. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Artemisia sacrorum subsp. iwayomogi include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and relieve menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve circulation. In traditional Japanese and Korean herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
